featuremap_caption: :material/star: **FeatureMap:** Displays the time and mass distribution of features,Please select a range on the graph for the next step
integration_caption: :material/star: **Integration:** Displays the integrated spectrum within the selected area
ptms_caption: :material/star: **PTMs Matching:** Displays the PTMs matching results within the selected time range
integration_no_data_warning: No valid data in the integration interval, please adjust the range settings
ppm_threshold_help: If the accuracy exceeds this threshold, it is considered to exceed the mass spectrometry accuracy tolerance range, and there is no matching modification.
fullscreen_tip: Tip: Click the top-right button of the table to enter fullscreen mode
exbasy_text: 📃 You can use the ExBasy tool to calculate the approximate mass of your target protein.
exbasy_tip: ❗ For high-resolution mass spectrometry, you need to click the 'Monoisotopic' option to calculate.
no_valid_data_warning: No valid data within the selected range.
no_near_peaks_warning: No neighboring peaks found within the specified range.
data_limit_help: Sort by intensity from high to low, display the top n strongest points to clarify the image
custom_color_help: If enabled, it will override the default color configuration
integration_manual_checkbox: Click To Set Integration Range Mannually
integration_manual_help: If you are unsatisfied with the automatic integration range, you can set the integration range mannually.
near_peak_expander: **Neighbor Peaks Filter:** Use The Highest Peak As The Base Peak
intensity_threshold_help: If the intensity of the neighboring peak is higher than this threshold, it will be considered as a neighbor peak.
near_peak_help: ❗ For high-resolution mass spectrometry, you need to click the 'Monoisotopic' option to calculate.
unimode_text: use the link below to search PTMs in the unimode database
batch_input_PTMs_help: ❗ For high-resolution mass spectrometry, you need to click the 'Monoisotopic' option to calculate.
featureid_input_help: Input the FeatureID that interests you
prsm_link_help: ❗ For high-resolution mass spectrometry, you need to click the 'Monoisotopic' option to calculate.
